title: They'll remember the stories.
lines: You were tired every single night.
You read the long one anyway. You did the voices anyway.
They won't remember the laundry that didn't get folded. They'll remember you, on the floor, doing the voices.

title: You held it together. Barely. That counts.
lines: Everyone got fed. Everyone got to bed. The work got done, mostly.
Nobody saw what it cost you, because you made sure of that.
You are running on fumes and love. It is not sustainable. It held, this week.

title: Something quiet went missing this week.
lines: You were in the room. You weren't always there.
They stopped asking you to watch somewhere around Wednesday.
Nothing broke. Nothing you could point to. Just a small, quiet distance, the kind that grows if you let it.

title: Enough.
lines: Some nights you were the parent you meant to be. Some nights you weren't.
You traded, and traded, and there was never quite enough of you to go around.
You loved them through all of it. This week, that was enough. It usually has to be.
